subject: Geography
title: The London Underground Map
description: A 1-minute de-risking session on The London Underground Map.

hook.text: Harry Beck's 1931 Tube map wasn't geographically accurate—and that's why it worked.

buildup.text: He simplified the messy reality into clean straight lines and even spacing.

discovery.text: Riders didn't need geography. They needed to know which stop comes next.

twist.text: Transport officials initially rejected it. The public loved it instantly.

climax.text: Every metro map in the world now copies Beck's design principle.

punchline.text: The best map of London doesn't look like London at all.

quiz.question: What made Harry Beck's Tube map revolutionary?
options: It simplified geography into a schematic diagram
It was the first color map
